title: Mimamsa Sutra
edition_note: Pilot passages and grader notes only. Verify against the edition and commentary tradition you choose before public release.

passages.grader_use: Use for questions about the opening inquiry into dharma.

passages.translation_or_note: Dharma is the object characterized by Vedic injunction.
passages.grader_use: Use for grading source-grounded answers about dharma, injunction, and limits of ordinary pramanas.

passages.text: Mimamsa interpretation distinguishes injunctive passages from explanatory, narrative, praise, and blame passages, while often using the latter to support the force and application of injunctions.
passages.translation_or_note: This is a compact grader note, not a sutra quotation.
passages.grader_use: Use for grading vidhi and arthavada distinctions.

passages.text: Apurva is used in Mimamsa ritual theory to account for an unseen efficacy generated by ritual action that connects the act to a later result.
passages.translation_or_note: This is a compact grader note, not a sutra quotation.
passages.grader_use: Use for grading questions about ritual causality and unseen results.

passages.text: Before accepting contradiction, Mimamsa interpretation tests sentence connection, context, indicatory marks, proximity, repetition, naming, primary and secondary meaning, and the functional class of each passage.
passages.translation_or_note: This is a compact grader note, not a sutra quotation.
passages.grader_use: Use for grading applied hermeneutic reasoning.
